page_content:
HIST383 City God, Dragon Boat, and Monkey King: Popular Culture and Society in Imperial China (3 Credits) Explores popular culture, society, and religion in China, with a focus on the late imperial periods (1368-1911). Together we will read and discuss a variety of (translated) primary sources and secondary sources, including the famous sixteenth-century popular novel The Journey to the West, which features the adventures of the powerful Monkey King in his quest for pleasure, immorality, and salvation. We will raise and address questions about popular culture, vernacular literature, folk religion, ritual operas, and everyday life of ordinary people in late imperial China. Credit Only Granted for: HIST319A or HIST383. Formerly: HIST319A.

HIST384 Global History of Capitalism (3 Credits) Surveys the development of capitalism since the 1500s from a global perspective, focusing on Asia, Africa, and Latin America. Topics include markets, entrepreneurship, commodity flows, labor migration, conflicts between states and empires, and environmental impacts. Engagement with primary sources and key debates, tracking changing ideas and interpretations of capitalism over time. Credit Only Granted for: HIST329L or HIST384. Formerly: HIST329L.
